group1.name: Doug Lineberry
group1.title: Patent Attorney
group1.bio: Doug is a highly experienced intellectual property attorney with a technical background in biology and chemistry. He has built a strong practice focused on patent prosecution, trademark portfolio management, and IP litigation. Doug holds a B.S. in Biology and a B.A. in Chemistry from Emory & Henry University and earned his J.D. from Mercer University School of Law. His expertise spans mechanical, medical, and chemical technologies, which allows him to adeptly handle complex IP issues. Doug is known for his compassionate approach and clear communication with clients, fostering trust and long-term relationships.

group2.name: Christian Feilmeier
group2.title: President
group2.bio: Christian serves as the Managing Director of rpc and President of rpc US, where he leads global strategy at the intersection of transformation, performance, and talent development. He has spent his career leading change at the point where global strategy meets local reality. From boardrooms in Munich to dealer networks across Europe, North America, and Asia, Christian has worked with leaders navigating transformation in complex, highly competitive environments. Early in his career at the BMW Group, he learned that performance improvement isn’t driven by slogans or systems alone: it is driven by leadership that understands culture, incentives, and execution at every level of the organization. He brings a rare combination of financial discipline, strategic clarity, and hands-on operational leadership to organizations undergoing transformation. Christian has led some of the automotive industry’s most consequential initiatives, including playing a key role in the implementation of the agency sales model in Europe, helping redesign how vehicles are sold, priced, and delivered. He has driven digitalization efforts across dealer networks, supported AI-powered customer interaction strategies, and led the founding of new companies in Germany, the United States, and China, building complete leadership teams from the ground up. What defines Christian as both a leader and as a speaker is his international and intercultural perspective. Fluent in German, English, and French, he understands that leadership does not translate word-for-word across borders. It must be adapted, contextualized, and executed with empathy and precision. His keynotes help leaders understand how to balance innovation with discipline, global consistency with local flexibility, and technology with human connection. Transformation succeeds when strategy, culture, and execution move together—across borders, teams, and markets.

group2.name: Stephen Thomas
group2.title: Associate VP
group2.bio: Stephen Thomas has built his career at the intersection of strategy and execution – where bold ideas either gain traction or quietly stall. He specializes in solving the problems that most organizations feel, but struggle to name. Across consulting, retail performance, and large-scale transformation initiatives, he has helped leaders turn complex plans into clear action. Stephen serves as Associate Vice President at rpc USA, where he operationalizes modern talent ecosystems. He leads cross-functional teams and mission-critical initiatives, ensuring that recruitment, development, performance, and leadership strategy don’t operate in silos. His work translates vision into structured action, aligning people, process, and performance within the realities of time, budget, and capacity. His expertise spans project leadership, operational execution, client strategy, workforce development, and organizational alignment—skills sharpened over more than two decades inside consulting and performance-driven environments. Before stepping into executive leadership, Stephen spent over eighteen years advising organizations on performance improvement across the AEC, automotive, retail, and professional services sectors. What distinguishes Stephen as a speaker and leader is his ability to communicate complexity with clarity. With a background in Mass Communication, studies in Architecture and Building Sciences, and certification in Project Management, he brings both structural thinking and human insight to the stage, blending real-world execution lessons with practical frameworks leaders can apply immediately. Stephen is grounded, pragmatic, and deeply relatable. He speaks to leaders navigating growth, transformation, and execution pressure, offering practical frameworks to organize work, align teams, and build sustainable momentum without burning people out. His philosophy is simple: Great outcomes aren’t accidental. They’re designed, aligned, communicated, and executed—one decision at a time

group3.name: Phillip Baltazar
group3.title: Predictive Index Senior Advisor and Certified Line of Sight Implementer
group3.bio: For more than two decades, Philip Baltazar has worked at the intersection of strategy, leadership, and execution, where bold vision becomes reality. Today, he helps leaders turn complexity into clarity and momentum. As Founder and CEO of Reiden Group, he advises CEOs, founders, and private equity sponsors on enterprise growth, turnarounds, and value creation, delivering consistent20–30%profitability lifts by aligning human capital, operating models, and strategy execution. His experience spans financial services, fintech, real estate, and private equity–backed businesses, where he has led capital raises, guided M&A strategy, and helped organizations scale without losing their culture or their soul. Phillip built and scaled businesses from the ground up. He founded a specialized lending platform, raised capital, and captured underserved markets through borrower-first innovation. As SVP at New Day USA, he launched a division from zero to 100+ transactions per month, reaching profitability in under six months while building a high-performing team and national brand presence. Across prior executive roles, he helped originate over $8 billion in loan volume, launching direct-to-consumer platforms and strategic partnerships with companies like Costco and Google. What sets him apart is not just what he’s built, but how. As an executive advisor at Predictive Index, he worked with C-suite teams to reduce turnover and increase profitability, proving that culture, when measured and aligned, is not a soft concept: it’s a growth engine. A global citizen who has lived and worked across Europe, Africa, and the Americas, Phillip brings a rare perspective on leadership across cultures, markets, and economic cycles. He is also deeply committed to mentorship and service, supporting organizations that help young people build discipline, confidence, and direction

page1.Session: From Innovation to Adoption: How Suppliers Win When Value Is Aligned and Sequenced Correctly

group1.name: Dr. Berkley Baker
group1.title: Founder & CEO
group1.bio: Dr. Berkley Baker is a Professor of Practice in Entrepreneurship and Innovation at Georgia State University and the Managing Partner of CIMI Solutions, a consulting and leadership development firm focused on innovation adoption. He has worked with more than 100 executive teams across industries including automotive, healthcare, technology, and manufacturing, helping organizations translate innovation into measurable results. Berkley is the creator of the CFOSS® framework (Core, Financial, Operational, Strategic, and Social Value) and the author of Customer Adoption Reimagined. He holds a doctorate from Georgia State University’s Robinson College of Business, an MBA from Emory University’s Goizueta Business School, and a bachelor’s degree from the U.S. Military Academy at West Point.

page1.Session: Decision Making for Today's Global Leaders

group2.name: Dr. José Sagarnaga
group2.title: Professor & Global Management Discipline Coordinator
group2.bio: Dr. José A. Sagarnaga is an award-winning global business educator and a leading voice on cross-cultural leadership and purpose-driven management. He serves as a Clinical Associate Professor in the Department of Management at Clemson University, where he coordinates the Global Management discipline area. A 2025 TEDx Clemson U speaker, Dr. Sagarnaga is known for combining compelling storytelling with research-based insights. His TEDx Talk, “Finding Your Purpose One Small Action at a Time,” draws on a personal experience that led him to a transformative journey in Bangladesh, shaping his life’s commitment to global education and service. With over two decades of international experience spanning 52 countries and programs developed in 21 nations, he brings a uniquely global perspective to leadership development, organizational culture, and the future of work.

page1.Session: Small Business Resources for Navigating Uncertainty

group3.name: Melissa L. Lindler, MPA, PMP
group3.title: District Director, SC District Office
group3.bio: Melissa Lindler joined the U.S. Small Business Administration in January 2025 as District Director of the South Carolina District Office, where she oversees SBA operations statewide and works to ensure entrepreneurs and small businessowners have access to the tools, resources, and support needed to start, grow, and thrive. In this role, she also leads the delivery of SBA programs and cultivates strategic partnerships with lenders, resource partners, and key stakeholders across South Carolina. Throughout her career, Ms. Lindler has held senior leadership roles across the public and nonprofit sectors. Prior to SBA, she served as Chief of Staff for the Southeast Crescent Regional Commission (SCRC), a federal-state partnership authorized by Congress to advance economic and infrastructure development across a seven-state region encompassing 428 counties. She served as a strategic advisor to agency leadership, supporting regional development, job creation, and community investment initiatives. Ms. Lindler served as Director of the City of Columbia’s Office of Business Opportunities, where she led efforts to support small, minority-and women-owned businesses through technical assistance, access to capital, and supplier development initiatives. Her responsibilities included oversight of commercial revolving loan funds, disparity study implementation, small business training and technical assistance, commercial retention and redevelopment efforts. Earlier in her career, Ms. Lindler spent 12 years with the U.S. House of Representatives as Director of District Planning and Outreach, where she supported constituents with federal funding navigation, policy development related to education and youth, district operations, and district budget management. A native of Irmo, South Carolina, Ms. Lindler holds a Bachelor of Arts in Political Science and a Master of Public Administration from the University of South Carolina. She also holds a graduate certificate in public management from Indiana University–Purdue University Indianapolis, is a Project Management Professional (PMP), and a Furman University Riley Leadership Institute Fellow

group3.name: Earl Gregorich, CBA
group3.title: Outreach & Marketing Specialist
group3.bio: Earl is a USAF veteran, graduated with a BS degree from Wright State University, and has had a 20+year career with US Small Business Administration resource partners, consulting business owners and administering business and economic development programs. Earl serves the 20 Upstate counties of South Carolina and is experienced in providing guidance on SBA lending programs, government contracting programs, SBA disaster relief programs and business development training.

group4.name: Ramón Nieves-Lugo
group4.title: Principal and CEO
group4.bio: Ramón leverages his extensive expertise in branding, strategy, and marketing to champion the value of a multicultural approach for his clients. Born and raised in Puerto Rico, he possesses a deep understanding of the cultural dynamics required to build strong, meaningful relationships that elevate brands. He is also committed to giving back and strengthening his community, reflecting his broader goal of building a legacy. Ramón currently serves on the Board of Directors for Public Education Partners and the Prisma Health Upstate Foundation. He has also served on the Advisory Council for USC Upstate’s Johnson College of Business and Economics, as well as the Greenville Chamber of Commerce—one of South Carolina’s largest business organizations. In addition, he previously served as Chair of Fall for Greenville, the largest food and music festival in the Upstate. A proud graduate of Bob Jones University, Ramón also holds an MBA from Strayer University. He resides in Greenville with his wife and their two children.
